notes: Installs fine, launcher loads fine, game starts, changes resolution, then flashes black and quits. Disabling videos did not help. Creating a 32bit WinePrefix did not help.
title: TRON 2.0

notes: You need to download msvcirt.dll v5.0.0.7022 and put it in the game's directory. Then it works flawlessly.
title: TRON 2.0

notes: Just like the post below, all I had to do was copy msvcirt.dll into my game folder. You can download it from lots of places. the 32 bit version worked fine. The game works great on integrated graphics.
title: TRON 2.0

notes:
Had to install msvcirt.dll, as others have mentioned. Can't get gamepad controller to work. Biggest upset was erratic mouse control that was solved by 1) Running winecfg and enabling Virtual Desktop to match my desktop's resolution. 2) Installing the Killer App Mod and then setting Tron 2.0 to use my desktop's resolution.

There is something wrong with this game's mouse warping that is not solved by playing with Mouse Warp Override.
title: TRON 2.0

notes: Just like others, I needed msvcirt.dll to make the game run, then it's almost perfect. I did have problems with mouse, but according to the forum this happens on Windows as well; I solved that by lowering my mouse polling rate way down to 125 Hz, anything higher than that felt unplayable.
title: TRON 2.0

notes: the msvcirt.dll found in WINE, or with winetricks, or in Proton 4.2, does not work (it's only 4kB).  I had to boot up an old Windows machine, find the file in C:\windows\system32 (this one is 54kB), and ftp it to my linux box.  copy it to $HOME/.steam/steamapps/common/Tron\ 2.0/ and the game works beautifully.  I tried so many times to install from my physical media over the years.
title: TRON 2.0
